protocol.name: ext_input_trigger_action_v1
protocol.copyright:

protocol.description:
This protocol lets a client react to a global input action. An action is an activity (for example "play/pause" or "toggle-launcher") that a privileged client has bound to one or more input triggers, such as a key combination, using the `ext_input_trigger_registration_v1` protocol.
A client subscribes by passing a token to `ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1.get_input_trigger_action`. The token is issued by the compositor, delivered to the registering client, and then passed to interested clients out of band (for example through a desktop settings service).
For example, a media player reacting to the system "play/pause" action:
# obtain the action's token out of band (e.g. from a settings # service), then subscribe to the action manager = registry.bind(ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1) action = manager.get_input_trigger_action(token)
# toggle playback each time the action fires on action.begin(time, activation_token): player.toggle_play_pause()
# the action no longer exists; clean up on action.unavailable(): action.destroy()

interface.name: ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1

description.summary: used to register interest in input actions

interface.description: This global interface allows a Wayland client to register interest in input trigger actions. It is advertised by the registry and bound by the client.

entry.name: invalid_token
entry.summary: token was not issued by the compositor or has been invalidated

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy input trigger action manager object

request.description: This informs the compositor that the input trigger action manager object will no longer be used. Existing objects created through this interface remain valid.

request.name: get_input_trigger_action

description.summary: register interest in input trigger action

request.description:
This request creates an input trigger action object.
The token should be one issued by the compositor. If the token was not issued by the compositor, or is no longer valid, the compositor must raise an `invalid_token` protocol error.
If the token has been issued by the compositor, is still valid, but is no longer available, then an `ext_input_trigger_action_v1.unavailable` event should be sent immediately.

interface.name: ext_input_trigger_action_v1

description.summary: a subscription to an input trigger action

interface.description:
This object is a client's handle to an input trigger action, obtained by passing an action token to `ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1.get_input_trigger_action`.
The compositor notifies the client each time the associated action fires: `begin` and `end` are always sent in pairs, with `begin` always preceding `end`.
If the action becomes unavailable the compositor sends the `unavailable` event, after which the object is of no further use and should be destroyed.

event.name: begin
event.description:
One of the input triggers associated with the action has occurred.
The client may begin the activity it associates with the action.
For keyboard triggers, this will be sent when all keys in the corresponding trigger have been pressed.
The time argument is a timestamp with millisecond granularity, with an undefined base. clients must not assume any relation to wall-clock time; timestamps are only valid for comparison/deltas.
The activation token argument is a short-lived opaque token that may be used with `xdg_activation_v1` to request to focus another surface.

event.name: end
event.description:
The input trigger that led to the "begin" event has stopped.
The client may end (or begin!) the activity it associates with the action.
For keyboard triggers, this will be sent when any key in the corresponding trigger has been released.
The time argument is a timestamp with millisecond granularity, with an undefined base. clients must not assume any relation to wall-clock time; timestamps are only valid for comparison/deltas.
The activation token argument is a short-lived opaque token that may be used with `xdg_activation_v1` to request to focus another surface.

event.name: unavailable

description.summary: the input action is no longer available

event.description:
The action this object refers to is, or has become, unavailable, so no begin or end events will be delivered. For example, the provider may have purged the action with `ext_input_trigger_action_control_v1.cancel_and_destroy`, or the token may already have been unavailable when this object was created (see `ext_input_trigger_action_manager_v1.get_input_trigger_action`).
This object is of no further use and should be destroyed.

request.name: destroy
request.description: This informs the compositor that the input trigger action will no longer be used.
